title: Can Partition into Equal Sum Subsets
description:
Determine if you can partition nums into two subsets such that the sums are equal. Return True or False.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ n ≤ 200
- 1 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 100
explanation:
Intuition
Subset-sum to target=sum(nums)/2 if total is even. Use 1-D boolean DP where dp[t] means achievable. Iterate numbers and update backwards to avoid reuse.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Compute total and target=total//2
2. Main Algorithm: Initialize dp[0]=True; for each x in nums, for t from target down to x set dp[t]
3. Return Result: Return dp[target]

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n·target)
- Space Complexity: O(target)

Key Insights
- Backward loop ensures each number used once
- Quick reject when total odd

Common Pitfalls
- Forward loop wrongly allows reuse
- Early exit when dp[target] becomes True is optional

testCases.explanation: When can_partition(nums=[1,5,11,5]), the expected result is true
When can_partition(nums=[1,1]), the expected result is true
